review_title: MSP Help Desk Manager Sentinel One Experience
description: As it was my first cyber security platform I really enjoyed the threat response, although there was never a major incident, we had minor items that were able to be resolved quickly.
author: Harrison K.
pros: I liked how easy it was to learn the platform, coming from a business where I did not a similar platform transitioning was easy.
cons: I do not remember having any negative feedback with the platform at the time I was using it because I had not used a platform like it previously.

review_title: A quick honest opinion of Sentinel One
description: The program works very well for an EDR. The AI once in a while has a false positive, but it is very easy to rectify. The client also dosent over load system processes. Very light weight.
author: Christopher S.
pros: Ease of use , is the main reason we love the platform. It is very easy to intagrate in to modern RMM platforms to deploy the client.
cons: It is a little dificult to import customers at times. Also setting up the scanning profiles for the first time is a little challenging.

description: Overall S1 is a fine product, and I prefer it to other antivirus solutions.
author: Colter W.
pros: Possible malware is detected immediately, and an alert is sent very soon after discovery.
cons: S1 has a tendency to obliterate a PC's resources when it runs a scan, which is unacceptable for older computers still in production.

review_title: SentinelOne A/V, MDR, XDR, EDR, Etc.
description: I have had a wonderful experience and would recommend it to friends and colleagues. I've so far had very little in the way of false positives with my clients, and everyone has been happy with it.
author: Chris D.
pros: Wonderful Management Portal, Easy To Use, Implemented it with my PSA, SuperOps, Low Memory Footprint, and Storage.
cons: Extremely hard to uninstall if the endpoint is deleted from the Management Portal. Other than this, I love everything about the product.

author: Michael S.
pros: We love the simplicity of the administrator platform and the protection that the suite offers to our clients.
cons: The support that we get from the MDR team is not the best. That is definitely the biggest area of improvement.

description: It has been a very positive experience using the product and we have a greater depth of knowledge of threats since using it.
author: Steve L.
pros: Its ability to detect threats and network disconnect the device to protect it and other devices on the network.
cons: I dislike that agents that are auto removed from the console cannot be uninstalled from the device without a lot of hassle.

description: Very positive experience. I do not have to think too much about it. Review the dashboard and make sure all my clients are running smoothly.
author: Paul B.
pros: The level of protection it provides my clients is amazing. I have watched it in real time restore a client's encrypted files (ransomware related encryption). Amazing!
cons: The first version of the product was very limited but we moved to a standalone system (verses integrated with N-Central) and our options are much better. I recently had to approve it tagging Norton VPN and it would not approve the entire string of hits. Another issue I have had is corrupted installs giving me trouble.

review_title: Great Product to protect your network
author: Scott B.
pros: Easy to use product, and installation of the agent is easy. Its effective protection against malicious threats.
cons: Its not easy to understand the different levels of this product and what each one adds to the product.

review_title: Easy to Manage, Very Attractive UI
description: it has been great, been with Sentinel one for a little over 6 months, moved away from bitdefender and the performance of the machines are much better.
author: Jarrett K.
pros: Easy to Deploy, Easy to Manage, Very Attractive UI and "pane-of-glass"
cons: more difficult to deploy and manage Apple devices compared to windows PCs

author: Jacob W.
pros: It runs in the background and does all the work in the background. Security is very important, and I trust these guys.
cons: Nothing I dislike. I never have to worry or think about an attack happening. I have full confidence they'll have my back.

review_title: Endpoint Detection and Response that delivers what it promises.
description: Very positive, I am made aware of version changes, issues, threats, and configuration problems quickly, which allows me to address these issues immediately. I have been very impressed by this product and by their support.
author: Russell C.
pros: The pricing is good for the quality of product you're purchasing, the detection is solid and picks up where other products might miss. Configuration and setup was intuitive and easy. Support has always been on top of whatever issues I have and resolves them quickly.
cons: So far I have only had minor issues which their support has been quick to address. Thankfully we have had no major issues with it and it is working like it should!

description: Overall, its best antivirus and we can rely on it up to certain point.
author: Romil T.
pros: It stops almost all malwares and also we can remote in into the device. We can send broadcast messages which I really like.
cons: It gives many false positives which sometimes are annoying.

description: Excellent so far, and a great approach with everything that was done for us. The reseller and the company went the extra mile to ensure all our questions were answered.
author: Mark O.
pros: Excellent deployment, great team follow up with weekly deployment schedules.
cons: Active Directory Ranger is not fully integrated.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: Presales was great. No pressure purchase. Answered all our questions. Gave several demos. Support has been great. No major issues during deployment. Updates have gone relatively smooth. Regular communication about updates and new features.
author: Chris M.
pros: Easy to deploy. Agent is lightweight. Locked down our environment - internal pen-testers have trouble bypassing it. Has saved us a few times as users will be users and click on anything and everything. Has been rock solid for the past year. Happy with the purchase.
cons: Sometimes it blocks things and it's tough to tell unless you really dig into it. Sometimes causes legit software to stop working until you whitelist. No automatic update for agents.

description: We absolutely love the solution. From planning to implementation, the software and support staff have been really responsive and great.
author: Jonathan P.
pros: I really like the ease of use with the software and how accurate it all is. It makes our daily IT lives a lot easier.
cons: The cost of the product is a bit much for the upper tiers, but overall justifiable to some.

description: It does what we want it to do, which is protecting our endpoints. Its a lot better than legacy AVs.
author: Brandon P.
pros: Very easy to use. Installation steps are easy to follow.
cons: When whitelisting something that was flagged, it does something weird to the file path. The actual file path isn't whitelisted. You have to go in and manually change it.

description: Overall it does what we need. It is a reliable EDR solution at a good price.
author: Andrew J.
pros: It is a great EDR solution we can integrate with our SOC. There is a lot of control with S1.
cons: The false alerts. Can be exhausting. We started to have excessive false isolations lately.

review_title: Reliable Protection with Room for Improvement
description: Overall, SentinelOne has been a reliable and effective security solution. It gives us strong protection with minimal effort, and once we got used to the interface, it became a key part of our cybersecurity stack.
author: Derek D.
pros: Strong threat detection, automatic remediation, easy deployment, and a clear dashboard that makes managing endpoints simple.
cons: While SentinelOne offers excellent protection, the interface can be a bit complex, especially for new users. It takes some time to get comfortable navigating through the different features. Additionally, the reporting features could be more flexible, as customizing reports to match specific needs is not always straightforward.

author: Chris H.
pros: I like the detection response and the way it behaves. It doesn't just scan for malicious files, it looks for malicious behavior. It has protected clients/users from actual attacks.
cons: Sometimes it does it's job too well, such as if you are just clearing out shadow copy data. But...things like that can be done in a malicious attack, so it's worth having to temporarily disable it from time to time. The updates fail sometimes and require a complete reinstall of SentinelOne and it can get a little difficult, sometimes requiring to use the cleaner tool and get into safe mode.

description: Over all I am impressed with SentinelOne. They are continually looking at what they do and asking "Is there more that we can do with this data?" Not all of the ideas were ones we use, but they keep looking at ways to be more useful. In that process they have not lost view of the core objectives to find the bad software, its bad behavior, and kill it. If something is questionable it can track what it doe so that you can reverse it all later.
author: Curtis S.
pros: The AI and behavior based monitoring as well as the normal fingerprint. Visibility give you the ability to look at process story lines and see how a process was triggered.
cons: There are some additional functions (like rogue devices) that sounded really neat, but they are not as useful to us as we thought they would be. We get too many notifications for them to be effective.

review_title: Great product - it’s too good sometimes but not too annoying
author: Chris H.
pros: It monitors behavior, it thinks some legitimate software and commands are malicious, but also easy to reverse and continue on. Lots of options for whitelisting. Not easy to be removed, so it’s not easy to get around.
cons: Sometimes I wish I could do VSS maintenance without having to disable the agent, but viruses and malware can attack VSS so I’m fine with it. Have had some issues with duplicating agents in the management side and trouble with updates.

review_title: This is not just about marketing but real protection against the most complex threats.
description: We've tested this solution a lot and we've assumed it is the best for us and for our customer. The agent that you install on servers or endpoints is lightweight and does not use so many resources that it slows down the system unlink other competitors. Moreover this solution doesn't produce so many false positive but all events ("incidents") are real events to investigate. In addition to this, the solution natively integrates with other products (not only for this brand) and offers an extensive API that you can use for integrating with custom applications (eg. Security Operation Center frameworks).
author: Simone C.
pros: Lightweight agent. Low false-positive rate. API and integrations.
cons: Not yet integrated mobile platform into the XDR platform.

description: After reviewing many different Endpoint Protection software, we found Sentinel One to have everything we needed. I've been impressed with it and would definitely recommend it to anyone.
author: Eric W.
pros: This software is hard to circumvent. I love the quarantine/block options on nefarious files. The notifications work great and there's many options to configure. It's quite simple to manage and makes my job so much easier.
cons: Sometimes the agent won't update on a PC. So it has to be manually uninstalledre-installed with special commands from SAFE MODE on Windows PCs. It's uncommon but it does happen.

description: It's been great in most cases
author: Pratik S.
pros: I like its Reporting, Monitoring and Alerting capabilities
cons: I think from an investigative standpoint, the deep dive search capability can be further improved.

review_title: SentinelOne does great job for protection and remediation
description: It does a great job protecting, containing and remediation but could be a little easier to administer.
author: Tom S.
pros: Ease of deployment, reporting and containment.
cons: As an administrator the web interface could be a little easier to remediate and disable agents when necessary.
